title: Market Brief — Saturday, April 11, 2026
summary: H100 availability sits at a severe 13% (131/355 GPUs), H200 at 26% (101/376), and B200 at a comfortable 41% (128/417), painting a two-tier market where last-gen silicon is critically scarce while Blackwell supply loosens fast. The biggest overnight move: H100 spot surged 20.9% to $1.87/hr even as H200 dropped 14.9% to $2.58, signaling legacy workload lock-in and a migration bottleneck away from Hopper.
paragraphs: H100 availability sits at a severe 13% (131/355 GPUs), H200 at 26% (101/376), and B200 at a comfortable 41% (128/417), painting a two-tier market where last-gen silicon is critically scarce while Blackwell supply loosens fast. The biggest overnight move: H100 spot surged 20.9% to $1.87/hr even as H200 dropped 14.9% to $2.58, signaling legacy workload lock-in and a migration bottleneck away from Hopper.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $6.83/M on H100, $6.19/M on H200, and $4.00/M on B200, meaning anyone selling at medium-tier pricing ($3/M) is underwater everywhere except B200 where losses narrow to $1.00/M per token. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral (margin-rich o3 Pro subsidizes aggressive GPT-5 Mini at $2/M output), Anthropic Strong (Opus at $25/M prints money), Google Neutral (Flash at $2.50/M flirts with breakeven), DeepSeek Burning Cash (V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at an estimated loss of $3.62/M tokens on H200).

B200 at 96.8% annualized price volatility and 41% availability is overvalued for spot buyers who can wait 30 days for further supply normalization. H200 at $2.58 spot after a 14.9% single-day drop with utilization climbing 7.9pp monthly is the undervalued compute play, so rotate H100 inference workloads to H200 now before the migration wave reprices availability.
bullets: 131 of 355 H100 GPUs available, a severe 13% availability rate signaling acute scarcity.
101 of 376 H200 GPUs available at 26%, just crossing out of the scarcity zone.
$0.38/M output makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest frontier-adjacent model, priced 99.5% below the LLMflation base.
96.8% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes Blackwell the riskiest compute asset to hold short-term.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token costs have fallen roughly 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2, the widest spread in the market.
